_article: XXXIX.7 — Influence Map is a first-class compiled artifact. XXXIV — faction relationships. User directive: 'Influence Map' as a compiler output.
_description: Schema for the COMPILED Influence Map — a spatial overlay of faction influence (and disputed zones) across the world. Each faction projects influence from its strongholds; influence decays with distance, terrain, and rival influence. Disputed zones are where two factions' influence overlaps. The simulation queries this to answer 'who controls this region?' and 'where could a conflict ignite?'.

factions.faction_class: enum — sect | dynasty | clan | bandit_confederation | merchant_guild | beast_court | rogue_cultivator_network

influence_sources.stronghold_id: string — the entity from which influence projects

regions.region_id: string — a spatial cell (chunk-aligned or province-aligned)
regions.controlling_faction: string|null — the faction with the highest influence here (null = unclaimed wilderness)
regions.disputed: bool — true if two+ factions have influence > dispute_threshold (conflict ignition risk)
regions.dispute_severity: float 0.0–1.0 — how contested (drives faction_grudge_momentum flashpoint proximity)

indices.by_faction: map<faction_id, [region_id]> — what regions does this faction control/dispute

queries_supported: contested_between(faction_a, faction_b) -> [region_id] — where their influence overlaps

_compile_source: World Blueprint DSL v2 — Faction entities + governs/claims/allied_with/at_war_with relationships + stronghold positions
_runtime_consumers: Conflict system (disputed zones are ignition points)
